reviews.author: Pratap Narayan
reviews.text: Our family of 5 visited Vietnam on a 7 night, 8 day visit. Our 2nd stop in this beautiful country was when we arrived to Da Nang by flight from Ho Chi Minh city. Our daughter Neha had planned our whole trip. A must visit our daughter had told us was the Golden Bridge its a 150-meter-long pedestrian bridge in Sun World Ba Na Hills near Da Nang, Vietnam, famous for the two massive, weathered stone hands appearing to lift the golden walkway. Its beautiful indeed. She insisted we start early as it would get really crowded as its highly popular. Situated 1,400 meters above sea level, it offers a panoramic mountain views, often floating above the clouds. We took a lot of pictures and walked down this amazing bridge. It will remain etched in our memories for long i am sure. A must visit when in Da Nang Vietnam. I assure you you will love it. Dr. Narayan Pratap bangalore India

reviews.author: Linford D'Souza
reviews.text: Visiting the Golden Bridge at Sun World Ba Na Hills was one of those experiences that instantly feels surreal. Located in the mountains above Da Nang, the bridge looks like something out of a fantasy movie, with giant stone hands appearing to hold the walkway in the air. The design, the elevation, and the surrounding misty hills make the entire place incredibly beautiful and extremely photogenic. Every angle feels like a postcard. The curved golden walkway contrasts perfectly with the greenery and clouds around it, making it one of the most unique architectural attractions I’ve seen. If you enjoy photography or simply love visually stunning places, this spot will definitely impress you. However, when I visited, it was very crowded. There were large groups of tourists all trying to take photos at the same spots, which made it a little difficult to properly enjoy the bridge or get uninterrupted pictures. At times it felt like you only had a few seconds before the next group stepped in. Because of that, the experience can feel slightly rushed. That said, it’s still absolutely worth visiting. The structure itself is stunning and truly memorable. If you are traveling to Vietnam, this is definitely something you’ll want to tick off your travel bucket list. Just try to go early in the morning if you want better photos and a more relaxed experience.

reviews.author: Earth Wanderer
reviews.text: Golden Bridge (Cầu Vàng) that captures the magic of the location. A Surreal Masterpiece – The Absolute Highlight of Vietnam! If you are coming to Vietnam, the Golden Bridge is a non-negotiable must-visit. Seeing the giant stone hands holding up the gold thread of a bridge amidst the clouds is an experience I will never forget. The View "Mesmerizing" doesn't even begin to describe it. The architecture is stunning, but the panoramic views of the mountains and the sea in the distance are what truly take your breath away. Walking across it feels like walking through a fantasy world. More Than Just a Bridge While the bridge is the star, the entire Sun World Ba Na Hills complex offers so much more: * The Cable Car ride to get up there holds world records and offers incredible scenery. * The French Village is beautiful for photos. * There are lovely flower gardens and plenty of entertainment options. Final Verdict It lives up to the hype and is worth every penny. Make sure your camera is charged because you won't want to stop taking photos!

reviews.author: Utkersh Bora ubmuse
reviews.text: Imagine stepping into a place where your legs say “I’m tired”, your camera says “One more photo!”, and your brain says “What even is this place?!” — welcome to the Golden Bridge area at VinWonders Phu Quoc. First things first: it’s STUNNING. Like “Did I accidentally walk into a fantasy movie set?” stunning. The bridge and surrounding architecture look so dramatic that even your most boring pose suddenly screams main character energy. You don’t walk here — you strut, preferably in slow motion. Now the fun part. VinWonders is MASSIVE. And by massive, I mean “I questioned my life choices and my calf muscles” massive. You’ll walk, walk some more, stop for photos, walk again, and then look at the map and laugh nervously because you’ve covered approximately 3% of the park. The vibes? A delightful mix of wow, why is it so hot, and okay fine, one more selfie. The crowd energy is enthusiastic, chaotic, and occasionally confused — like everyone collectively forgot why they came here but decided to enjoy it anyway. Pro tip: hydrate like you’re training for the Olympics, wear comfy shoes (fashion will betray you here), and embrace the madness. This place isn’t about perfection — it’s about fun, fantasy, and pretending you’re not tired.

reviews.author: Chris Donaldson
reviews.text: I am from Australia and this is a must visit location, the place is magical. Photos can be a little challenging with the volume of people that come here, recommend coming here earlier for the easier good photos. The afternoon is also not bad. Fantastic staff always being helpful and there are plenty of bathrooms and food/drink stalls to keep you feeling comfortable at all times.

reviews.author: George
reviews.text: Really great place. The cable car was a bit scary for me but every one else seemed fine. It will take a full day to get round. If you get offered golden tickets before you get on the cable car, take then because they are beer/food vouchers for the beer bar by the amusements. Enjoy!

reviews.author: Babyblue
reviews.text: Just visited the Golden Bridge for the first time, and it was absolutely breathtaking! The giant stone hands are even more impressive in person. ☁️✨ I truly loved the view, but I have to be honest—the cable car ride was a bit of a challenge for me. I get quite motion sick, so the long ride made me feel really dizzy. 🤢 However, once I stepped onto the bridge, the experience made it all worth it! Highly recommended for the views, but maybe bring some motion sickness pills if you're like me!

reviews.author: abhishek bharati
reviews.text: This was a hidden gem for me, i only went here thinking of the hand bridge. But dyumm, this place was crazy good. The cable car goes through a really pretty scenery including over river and a waterfall. The amusement park on the top is really nice. Rides here are not expensive and fun.

reviews.author: Nails and Art by Gıe
reviews.text: A must visit in Da nang. You need to book ticket online for booking in advance. You can pay also additional for VIP pass once you’re in the gate (it’s optional that your tour guide will ask you) to avoid the queue. I booked our tour thru Viator application a week before our travel.The buffet was amazing, there’s a free beer also but unfortunately we didn’t have enough time to taste the beer. It was a wonderland experience.

reviews.author: Ankita Arya
reviews.text: The Golden Bridge in Da Nang is truly breathtaking! The iconic design with the giant stone hands holding the bridge is absolutely stunning. The location and management are top-notch, and the views from the bridge are spectacular, especially when the clouds roll in and then clear up, revealing the lush jungle and mountains below. It’s a bit crowded, as expected for such a famous spot in Vietnam, but the atmosphere is amazing. The staff is friendly, the security is excellent, and everything is well-managed. It’s definitely a must-visit destination when you’re in Da Nang!

reviews.author: Z M
reviews.text: The views from the Golden Bridge are absolutely spectacular. It's literally above the clouds at an altitude of 1400 meter, about 4600 feet above sea level in Bà Nà Hills. Its structured to look like it's been held by two giant hands. Its inside the the amusement park which is amazing on its own. It's free to visit, the price is included in the general admission ticket which cost 1 million Dong. You take the cable ride up to the mountain and it feels like you're entering Jurassic Park world. Very cool experience all around. Must visit if you make it to Da Nang. About a hour ride on a scooter or you can book a tour. Transportation included. Highly recommended 👌

reviews.author: Syrristen
reviews.text: A very good experience! There are so much stuff to see up there other than the bridge. There was a beautiful flower garden, a pagoda, a huge Buddha statue and more. The cablecar to get up to the top is very high quality and modern, and it felt safe the entire way. At the top there are some shops for food, warm clothes and other souvernirs. It felt a little bit like a theme park 😆 But it was great! Both me and my girlfriend really enjoyed it, and we got some great pictures (of course 😝) Definetly recommend this spot if you're within a few hours of driving time. There are loads of drivers you can hire for the day, and even Grab could work.

reviews.author: Anilgowda
reviews.text: Golden Bridge in Da Nang is a must-visit attraction and truly lives up to the hype. Reaching Ba Na Hills and the Golden Bridge by cable car makes the experience even more spectacular, with breathtaking views along the way. The bridge looks stunning, and you can capture amazing photos with the iconic giant stone hands. They have displayed almost all national flags, and visitors can pick their country’s flag and pose proudly—this adds a very nice and unique touch to the experience. Overall, a beautiful place with an unforgettable atmosphere and perfect photo opportunities. Best to visit early morning to avoid crowds and enjoy the cool misty weather.

reviews.author: Maxine Harrison
reviews.text: Definitely check the weather up there before you book, I was going from the Da nang forecast which was totally different to Ba Na hills. I went in the afternoon as it was sold as quieter and a great sunset but it was throwing it down so could hardly see anything at all. It would be great on a fine day. I just got the tickets online which included buffet lunch and free shuttle to and from Da Nang. The buffet lunch had lots of choice

reviews.author: Eason Chen
reviews.text: A very special landscape spot on Banahill park. The most famous is the two huge hands holding the golden bridge. And I recommend must need to check the weather before you come. Better choose the sunny day ! Can see all the view from the mountains side.

reviews.author: Prakash Natarajan
reviews.text: It is one of the best lifetime experience to walk through a bridge virtually hanging in the sky. Marvelous view, excellent weather. Good signage. Stunning design of two giant stone hands supporting the bridge. Breathtaking panoramic views of the landscapes. The cable car ride is an unforgettable experience soaring above the treetops.

reviews.author: Yvonne De'Mille
reviews.text: A MUST DO! But definitely book accommodation and stay a night or 2 as you get access to the bridge without all the people and there is so much to see and do here. A wonderful experience you will not regret.🇻🇳🙌 Also when you book accommodation entry for the cable car is discounted at check in.

reviews.author: J T
reviews.text: The Golden Bridge at Ba Na Hills in Da Nang was absolutely stunning. Walking across the bridge held up by the giant stone hands was such a unique and unforgettable experience. The views of the mountains and surrounding landscape were breathtaking, especially in the cool morning mist. The area was well maintained and beautifully designed, making it perfect for photos. Definitely a must-visit attraction when in Da Nang! Check the weather before you go as it could be really misty. Plan your visit here either early morning or around 4 pm when the crowd is less. I would recommend buying the wow pass as it gives priority access to the cable cars.

reviews.author: Len Ng
reviews.text: One of the main attractions on Ba na hill. Good to be there earlier in the day. Later mists might appear and make photography a challenge.

reviews.author: A K
reviews.text: Took a flurry of photos like a kid in a chocolate factory. The size of this place is colossal. You have to actually see it to appreciate the size and beauty of it all and I believe the developers have done an amazing job. There is vast construction still taking place and the topic of conversation had to be…’ so how do they get all these materials up here?’ The cable cars were in crazy numbers and very well maintained. The chain of pods never seemed to end and they were not just in one silence but at least 3!!. Cleaned to a high spec and felt safe always. The height is deceiving from the bottom until you get into the cable cars and the view is breathtaking, especially as you enter into the clouds. The bridge itself is covered in the clouds and time to time when it clears up the size is unveiled. Huge respect to the detail of the project. I believe the prices are the best I have seen anywhere in a few years in can this going up exponentially. I would absolutely recommend a visit to this site. We bought basic tickets from the vendor en route which cost us 950k plus the compulsory 3% charges. If ever nearby don’t go home without a visit.

reviews.author: SUMEET VERMA
reviews.text: The best way to get the glimpse of this place or if you want a no fuss picture of yours then i recommend staying at the mercure bana hills and arrive as early as possible. The first cable car leaves at 6 in the morning and as the day progresses the crowd grows more and more. Tip: keep your roats and umbrellas handy. I stayed there for 3 days and the weather was all cloudy and rainy.just a glimpse of the weather.😀

reviews.author: Ruidz
reviews.text: We decided to do our own tour (sort of). We took a Grab (360k) and the driver asked if we needed a ride back so he waited and we paid the same but in cash to him. He took us to get ticket before arriving (1mil dong each). Then we took the cable car up but is was very foggy and raining so we couldn't see much. It was no different at the top and was freezing cold. So we decided to do the bridge and then go back to the city. A little expensive when you don't see much.

reviews.author: Yuk Ming Fong
reviews.text: We stayed at the hotel in Sun World Ba Na Hills so we got to take the early cable cars to Golden Bridge. We arrived there at 08:30am and there were already quite a few people. It was a cloudy day (near Christmas) and, as the Golden Bridge is on a hill, we were inside the clouds. It was not the best weather but the clouds were always moving so there were times when the sights were clearer.

reviews.author: CHANG-OH KIM
reviews.text: A must-visit if you visit Da Nang. We arrived directly at the top of Bani Hill by cable car, so finding way to the Golden Bridge was a bit of a challenge. we were eventually able to reach it by taking the another cable car down a short distance from the summit.

reviews.author: eric yeo
reviews.text: Must visit iconic scenic location, BaNa Sun World. Cooling weather, windy cold air during November December. Enjoyable and the multi cable car rides and a short tram ride is panoramic of such majestic highlands.

reviews.author: Gaurav Rane
reviews.text: Golden Bridge (Ba Na Hills), Vietnam – 5★ Review The Golden Bridge in Ba Na Hills, Vietnam, is an absolutely breathtaking attraction and truly deserves a 5-star rating. Famous for its iconic design, the bridge appears to be gently held by two massive stone hands, creating a surreal and magical visual that feels straight out of a fantasy world. Suspended high above the mountains, the views from the bridge are nothing short of spectacular. As you walk along the Golden Bridge, you’re surrounded by misty hills, lush greenery, and cool mountain air, making the experience both refreshing and awe-inspiring. The golden railing gleams beautifully under sunlight, while the clouds often float around the bridge, giving visitors the feeling of walking above the sky. It’s an unforgettable experience, especially for first-time visitors. The bridge is well-maintained and easily accessible via the Ba Na Hills cable car, which itself offers stunning panoramic views. Despite being popular and sometimes crowded, the atmosphere remains enjoyable due to the organized layout and scenic surroundings. Overall, the Golden Bridge is a masterpiece of architecture and nature combined. It’s a must-visit destination in Vietnam for photography lovers, nature enthusiasts, and anyone seeking a truly unique travel experience. 🌉✨

reviews.author: Reha Sehgal
reviews.text: I visited the Golden Bridge early in the morning, and even though it was raining a little, the place was not crowded at all, which made it a very peaceful experience. The view from the bridge was amazing, with the mist adding a magical touch. It was a bit chilly, so make sure to dress warmly, but overall it was a beautiful and memorable visit. Highly recommended if you want to enjoy the scenery without the crowds!

reviews.author: Kuya Shane
reviews.text: Busy & crowded major point of Da Nang. Crowd dies off at arounf 3pm according to staff. Cable cars is only way to get there as service roads are blocked off to public. Many great views, its a worthwhile trip. Decent buffet and grrst French architecture.ots of entertainment. Expect tourist prices, but still ok. Is a bit colder up in this mountain way up in the clouds, particularly at night (Dec/Jan)

reviews.author: שיר ים פסקה
reviews.text: I visited Golden Bridge and the place itself is absolutely stunning, the views and the bridge are unforgettable. Unfortunately, the experience was ruined by misleading ticket sales. At the ticket counter, the seller strongly pushed me to buy a ticket that included a buffet, claiming that all restaurants at the top were closed. I explicitly asked, using a translation app, to confirm that everything was closed up there, and she insisted it was true. What she did not say was that the buffet ends early. Later we discovered that food options were available, just not through the buffet ticket we were pushed to buy. The feeling of being scammed stayed with us the entire day and completely ruined the experience. Because of that, despite how beautiful the place is, I would not return and cannot fully recommend it. Transparency with visitors really matters.

reviews.author: Kazi Zaafer Alam
reviews.text: Must-See Landmark in Vietnam The Golden Bridge is truly a stunning and iconic attraction. The design is incredible — the giant stone hands holding the bridge make it feel like something straight out of a fantasy movie. The views from the top are beautiful, especially on a clear day. It was misty when we went, which added a nice mysterious feel. We visited during peak time, so it was quite crowded, and the foggy weather limited visibility a bit. Even so, the atmosphere was unique and somewhat magical. The cable car ride up to Ba Na Hills was also a highlight — smooth, scenic, and definitely worth experiencing. Just be prepared for crowds and a fair amount of walking, but it’s absolutely worth it. If you’re visiting Da Nang or Hoi An, the Golden Bridge is a must-visit attraction!
